summary.intent: Package the Gossamer Electron app for distribution via Homebrew, including bundling the server inside the app and automating releases via GitHub Actions.
summary.outcome: GitHub Actions release workflow was created and debugged to build macOS DMGs, publish to GitHub Releases, and auto-update the Homebrew tap formula. The workflow is now triggered on version tags and all configuration files are committed.

repo: The project has a monorepo structure: Electron app lives in app/, server (gossamer-serve) lives in root src/. They have separate package.json files and node_modules.
server.ts uses import.meta.url to resolve PROJECT_ROOT for Prisma binary paths — bundled layout must preserve this path structure.
prisma was in devDependencies but is needed at runtime for pushSchema — must be in production dependencies.
The Homebrew tap repo is at ../homebrew-gossamer (github.com/nosman/homebrew-gossamer), Casks live in Casks/ subdirectory.
The gossamer repo's default branch for active packaging work is README, not main.

code.finding: Main process was updated to spawn the server as a utilityProcess in production, gated by absence of ELECTRON_RENDERER_URL env var.
CJS wrapper created to bridge utilityProcess.fork() (CJS-only) to the ESM server via dynamic import().

Added electron-builder build config with extraResources to bundle server dist/, node_modules/, and prisma/ into Contents/Resources/server/. Also added repository field required by electron-builder for publish config detection.

Workflow uses --publish never flag on electron-builder (to prevent double-publish), then uses softprops/action-gh-release to upload DMGs. Includes a step to auto-update the Homebrew cask formula with real SHA256s and push to homebrew-gossamer using HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN secret.

workflow: electron-builder requires a repository field in package.json when run from a subdirectory — it cannot traverse up to find the .git root.
electron-builder's built-in publish and softprops/action-gh-release conflict — use --publish never and let softprops handle uploads.
GitHub Actions GITHUB_TOKEN defaults to read-only permissions; explicit permissions: contents: write is needed to create releases.
For fine-grained PATs writing to a separate tap repo, only Contents: Read and write + Metadata: Read-only are needed.
utilityProcess.fork() in Electron only accepts CJS entry points — ESM servers need a CJS wrapper that dynamically imports the ESM module.
npm ci fails if package-lock.json is out of sync with package.json — must commit updated lock file after adding new dependencies like electron-builder.

friction: package-lock.json was out of sync with package.json after adding electron-builder, causing npm ci to fail in CI.
electron-builder auto-detect of GitHub repo failed because .git is in the parent directory of app/ — required manually adding repository field.
electron-builder attempted to publish DMGs itself and conflicted with the softprops upload step — required adding --publish never flag.
Network outage during the session delayed pushing several commits, requiring manual push instructions.
Working on two branches (main and README) simultaneously caused confusion about which changes were committed where.
The GITHUB_TOKEN lacked permissions to create releases by default — required adding explicit permissions block to the workflow job.
open_items: Consolidating app/ and root src/ into a single package (intentionally deferred — meaningful refactor, not worth doing before first release).
App is unsigned — users will hit macOS Gatekeeper on first launch. Requires Apple Developer account ($99/yr) to resolve properly.
HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN secret must be added to gossamer repo Settings → Secrets before the cask auto-update step will work.
Need to revert workflow trigger from branch push back to v* tags — this was done but confirm it's on the correct branch.
gossamer-serve headless CLI use case: the bin entry could still be supported in package.json if standalone server use is desired.
